body.title: The universal level-contract + the OPEN question: what should the whole stack of levels/artifact-types be? (Akien+CC, 2026-07-10)
body.status: OPEN design dialogue — NOT settled. Candidate framing below is CC's proposed starting position for reconciliation WITH Akien, not a decision. Akien explicitly asked 'can you and I come to what the whole stack should be?' Parked to return to skills first.
body.text:
THE UNIVERSAL LEVEL-CONTRACT (Akien, 2026-07-10): 'every component's job really is to give enough information to the component below so it can do its job.' The human hands the top a pile of words; each level's sole obligation is to hand the level below ENOUGH to do its job. This is the TEST for whether the stack is right: a level earns its existence iff it produces a DISTINCT 'enough for the next consumer' artifact. Too many levels = a level that doesn't change the sufficiency threshold; too few = a level handed less than it needs. Pairs with 'intentions simplify as they go' — a well-formed level hands DOWN a tighter, smaller problem.

GROUNDING EXAMPLE — the INTERVIEWER intent (Akien, 2026-07-10; captured, NOT being built now): the Interviewer asks the kinds of questions that lead to answers like Akien's own rich self-clarifying ones. It is a CHATBOT with its own domain and an AGENTIC CHAT LOOP; contained LLM calls. Goal: raise GLOBAL intention to high-enough definition that it can be DECONSTRUCTED INTO SUB-INTENTIONS AND PROOFS. Tools: the base trainings all LLMs have, PLUS a made-up starting set of questions for first experiments; expect a lot of prompt-tuning (fine). The tools developed here are useful ALL THE WAY DOWN. Each component gets TESTED — even where no code is built, PROCESSES are built and tested. NOTE Akien already pairs sub-intentions AND proofs as the Interviewer's output — proof-thinking is born at the top, not bolted on late.

THE OPEN QUESTION Akien posed: right now we have INTENTIONS + DECISIONS + TICKETS. Is that the right set? (a) Do we need intentions AND designs (is 'design' a distinct layer)? (b) Do we need a layer that thinks about the PROOFS? He is explicit this is a joint design question, and that what he described is NOT claimed to be the whole stack.

CC's CANDIDATE FRAMING (proposed, for reconciliation — NOT settled):
  - Apply the sufficiency test to each proposed layer.
  - INTENTION (present-tense contract + why + how-to-verify + constraints) and DESIGN (the SHAPE that realizes the intention — declarative, like an architecture intention-point/concept) ARE distinct sufficiency thresholds -> keep both.
  - DECISION is probably NOT a mandatory standalone layer: a decision is a FORK RESOLVED (a committed choice among alternatives, with why + falsifiable hypothesis). It fires WITHIN design only where there is a real fork; uncontested shape needs no decision. So: decisions are events inside the design layer, not a station every artifact must pass through.
  - PROOF is NOT a separate late box either: the proof obligation is BORN WITH each (sub-)intention as its how-to-verify (Akien's Interviewer already emits sub-intentions ⊗ proofs as a pair), then SHARPENED at design (which proof discriminates THIS shape), LOCKED at prebuild (prereg firewall — already live), and EXECUTED at prove (L8). A sub-intention and its proof are the same act of deconstruction. So 'a layer that thinks about proofs' = YES as a CONCERN THAT ACCRETES ALONGSIDE INTENT, threading top-to-bottom, not one node.
  - CANDIDATE STACK (front half): Interview -> Deconstruct (sub-intentions ⊗ proof-obligations) -> Design (decisions = internal fork-resolutions) -> Ticket (carries sub-intention + proof-obligation + design constraints) -> [prebuild locks proof] -> [BUILD, out of scope] -> prove -> ... (back half per workflow-levels map).

FORCES A KNOWN RECONCILIATION (already flagged in project_intention_is_a_present_tense_contract): 'designs' is not yet an artifact type and the skills (/sorted, /ticket, /audit-*, /outcome) are DECISION-FIRST. Settling this stack means deciding whether DESIGN becomes a first-class artifact type and whether DECISION demotes to a fork-within-design. That reconciliation is the thing to converge on with Akien.

Related: architecture/cc.0.workflow-levels.*, note pipeline-walk-front-to-back, note staged-intention-compiler-pipeline, project_intention_is_a_present_tense_contract (designs-not-an-artifact-type reconciliation), architecture/cc.0.feedback-edges.*, D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20, project_build_packet_pre_inference_compiler (prereg locks the proof).
